name: Wheat
description: Wheat is one of the most important cereal crops in India, grown mainly in Punjab, Haryana, and Uttar Pradesh during the rabi season.

name: Rice
description: Rice is the staple food of India grown in flooded fields called paddies, mainly in West Bengal, Punjab, and Andhra Pradesh.

name: Maize
description: Maize is a versatile crop used as food, fodder, and raw material for industries. Grown widely in Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh, and Rajasthan.

name: Tomato
description: Tomato is one of the most widely grown vegetable crops in India, rich in vitamins and used extensively in cooking.

name: Potato
description: Potato is a major vegetable crop grown in Uttar Pradesh, West Bengal, and Bihar. It is a staple food and used in various cuisines.

name: Onion
description: Onion is an essential vegetable in Indian cooking, grown mainly in Maharashtra, Karnataka, and Madhya Pradesh.

name: Sugarcane
soilType: Deep loamy soil, rich in organic matter
description: Sugarcane is a major cash crop used for sugar production, grown mainly in Uttar Pradesh, Maharashtra, and Karnataka.

name: Cotton
soilType: Black cotton soil, deep and fertile
description: Cotton is a major cash crop used in textile industry, grown mainly in Gujarat, Maharashtra, and Telangana.

name: Chickpea
soilType: Sandy loam to clay loam soil
description: Chickpea is the most important pulse crop in India, rich in protein, grown mainly in Madhya Pradesh, Rajasthan, and Maharashtra.

name: Turmeric
soilType: Loamy or clay loam well-drained soil
description: Turmeric is an important spice and medicinal crop grown mainly in Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, and Tamil Nadu.

name: Mango
description: Mango is the national fruit of India, grown mainly in Uttar Pradesh, Andhra Pradesh, and Maharashtra. Known as the king of fruits.

name: Mustard
description: Mustard is a major oilseed crop grown in Rajasthan, Uttar Pradesh, and Haryana, used for cooking oil and condiments.

name: Lentil
description: Lentil is a highly nutritious pulse crop rich in protein and iron, grown mainly in Madhya Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, and Bihar.

name: Banana
soilType: Rich loamy soil with good drainage
description: Banana is one of the most widely consumed fruits in India, grown mainly in Tamil Nadu, Maharashtra, and Gujarat.

name: Soybean
description: Soybean is a protein-rich oilseed crop grown mainly in Madhya Pradesh, Maharashtra, and Rajasthan.
